characters.name: Al and Moo Sleech
characters.description: Al and Moo SleechBackground informationVoiceEddie KorbichCharacter informationFull nameAlbert "Al" Sleech / Mustafa "Moo" SleechAge11-12GenderMaleInterestsComputers, thinking, technology, the color purpleOccupationJunior high school studentsRelativesMr. Sleech (father)AlliesDoug Funnie,Skeeter Valentine,Patti Mayonnaise,Connie Benge,Beebe Bluff,Boomer Bledsoe.EnemiesRoger KlotzLikesTechnology, reading, earning moneyDislikesRogerFavorite ColorPurple[Source]Albert "Al"(who wears a flower-patterned shirt) andMustafa "Moo" Sleechare two prodigious twin brothers, who are both 11-year-old geniuses. They made their first appearance in the episodeDoug and the Weird Kids,in which Doug was paired with them to learn about their home life for a school report. They are both brainiacs, even going so far as making their own computer, called B.R.I.A.N., which they use to play games (such as Glorak!). Their father is a baker, a source of embarrassment for them, leading them to attempt to hide this from Doug. Since then, Doug has gone to them for technical help when needed and they have made various appearances. Doug once accused Al and Moo of stealing his prized bike. Not much is known about the twins' background, although it is confirmed that the two moved to Bluffington after Doug.

characters.name: Betty Bluff
characters.description: Betty BluffCharacter informationFull nameElizabeth BluffGenderFemaleInterestsMoneyRelativesBill Bluff(husband)Beebe Bluff(daughter)[Source] Betty Bluffis the mother ofBeebe Bluffand a minor character inDoug. She was first seen inDoug Wears Tights. Betty Bluff's first introduction was inDoug Wears Tights, where she runs the Bluffington Ballet and uses this to Beebe's advantage to get her the lead role inThe Beetcracker. After Beebe rushes her audition, she blames Doug for ruining her daughter's audition, but Beebe dismisses that accusation when she admits to doing it herself. She wanted to be costume designer, instead being involved with performance and explained that Betty "wanted it for her". After realizing her mistake, Betty apologizes to both Beebe and Doug.

characters.name: Bill Bluff
characters.description: Bill BluffBackground informationFirst episodeDoug Takes The CaseVoiceDoug PreisCharacter informationFull nameWilliam Hornblower Bluff IIIOther namesBilly BluffBirthdayJune 19GenderMaleInterestsMoneyOccupationBusinessmanRelativesBetty Bluff(Wife)Beebe Bluff(Daughter)[Source] Beebe Bluff(Daughter) William "Bill" Hornblower Bluff IIIis the wealthy, elitist father ofBeebe Bluff. He has a sharp business corporation sense and knows how to manage finances. Although Bill is not seen as a direct antagonist, he often puts money before other values, which makes him a villain in certain situations. Although generally an antagonist, he did help when Doug was given an internship at his company as part of a school project, saying that there are times in business and other aspects of life when difficult decisions must be made.

characters.description: Boomer BledsoeBackground informationVoiceBilly West(Nickelodeon)Chris Phillips(Disney)Character informationBirthday1980GenderMaleInterestsBullying, comic booksOccupationStudentAlliesRoger Klotz,Willie White,Ned Cauphee,Doug Funnie,Skeeter Valentine,Chalky Studebaker(sometimes)EnemiesDoug Funnie(sometimes)Favorite ColorYellow[Source]Boomer Bledsoeis a loyal friend ofRoger Klotz. He is approximately 11 1/2 years old. AlthoughDougdescribes him as "long on talk, short on brains," he isn't quite as dumb asWillie Whiteand is the nicest to Doug out of all of Roger's friends. Doug:(behind a coat rack to himself)This was it. I was certain of it. Boomer:(struggling with books)Doug?

characters.name: Cassandra Bleem
characters.description: Cassandra Bleemis a minor guest character of Disney'sDoug. She’s voiced by Melissa Greenspan. She’s a superficial eighth grade girl. She and her friends who appears in "Doug's Older Woman" whereDoug Funnieasked her on a date. However, during the date, Doug heard her say hurtful and mean things aboutPatti Mayonnaiseand realized she was bad news. Doug realized she only asked him out so he could make a fool out of himself in front of her friends. She’s by far, the most antagonistic character on the show. At the end of the episode, she dances with Al and Moo and thinks they're funny instead of Doug. Main Characters:Doug Funnie•Porkchop•Skeeter Valentine•Patti Mayonnaise•Roger Klotz

characters.name: Cecelia Newberry
characters.description: Cecelia NewberryCharacter informationOccupationSubstitute teacher[Source]Cecelia Newberryis a substitute teacher at theBluffington School. She substituted for the vacationingMrs. Wingo, who at that time was tending to her family. When she first came in, all the boy students fell in love, includingRoger. During her week on the job Ms. Newberry had to deal with Doug's alleged bad behavior; but she knows that deep down inside thatDougreally is a good kid.

He is wheelchair-bound, caused by the same accident that killed Patti's mother. An accident Chad caused by driving under the influence.

characters.name: Chalky Studebaker
characters.description: Chalky StudebakerCharacter informationFull nameChandler StudebakerBirthdayMarch 22, 1981 (age 40)GenderMaleInterestssports, academicsOccupationStudentRelativesClifford "Cliff" Studebaker(older brother)Mr. Studebaker (father)Mrs. Studebaker (mother)AlliesDoug Funnie,Skeeter Valentine,Patti Mayonnaise,Beebe BluffEnemiesRoger Klotz(sometimes)LikesSportsDislikesRoger[Source]Chalky Studebakeris a student atBluffington School, and also renowned as one of its best students. A popular student at Bluffington School, Chalky is a good-natured and all-around talented individual, who strives to be the best at everything he can be. Chalky lives at home with his father, Mr. Studebaker, a man whose wise advice has ensured the success of all of Chalky's endeavors thus far. But secretly, Chalky is trapped within in the shadow of his older unseen brother, Cliff, who has amassed and bettered all of Chalky's achievements in life. Chalky's sibling inferiority complex finally came to a head when his scheduled football practice interfered with his studies, and he failed to prepare for a test. Doug, who studied hard for the exam, is accused of cheating. Doug tries to explain he got the good grade fair and square, but is not believed on account of his somewhat checkered past, whereas Chalky is believed to be the star pupil who would never do anything wrong. In order to clear his name and avoid having to take the re-test, Doug attempts to hunt down Chalky and question him about the incident. This proves difficult, and Doug realizes that Chalky is avoiding him because of his guilt. Eventually tracking him to his house, Chalky desperately attempts to convince Doug to take the blame, citing all the times he had helped Doug in the past. Doug reluctantly agrees, and after an encounter with Mr. Studebaker, Doug learns of Chalky's secret war with his brother. While Mr. Studebaker is bragging to Doug about his son's achievements, a visibly annoyed Doug mumbles to himself that Chalky is a cheater. However, Mr. Studebaker overhears this comment and questions him about it, which causes Chalky to confess. Mr. Studebaker then realizes he may be putting too much pressure on Chalky; however he is also angry about the cheating and trying to make Doug the fall guy. Chalky eventually agrees to re-take the test, and sits out during the football game to do it.

characters.name: Chap Lipman
characters.description: Clyde "Chap" Lipmanis a member ofThe Beets. He plays the guitar. InDoug's Garage Band, Chap Lipman was seen on the television being interviewed. He explains that he got started by playing music in his house by hitting random objects. His mom would threaten to smack him if he continues, so he and his friends moved their jamming sessions to the shed. Eventually, they come up with"Killer Tofu".

characters.name: Charlene
characters.description: CharleneCharacter informationGenderFemaleOccupationTruck driver[Source] Charleneis a minor character that appears in the episodeDoug's Hot Ticket. She is a truck driverDougandSkeetermeet at theEat, Then Gas Diner. Charlene was initially seen as a crabby and bitter woman, as she complained after Doug and Skeeter kept talking aboutThe Beets. She mentions "Beetheads" bother her. She eventually warmed up to them and told them several stories about her exciting life as a truck driver. Before leaving she signs Skeeter's diner menu with the phrase "Clutch it".

characters.name: Connie Benge
characters.description: Connie BengeCharacter informationFull nameConstance BengeBirthdayJuly 27, 1981 (age 39)GenderFemaleOccupationStudentAlliesDoug Funnie,Patti Mayonnaise,Skeeter Valentine,Beebe Bluff,Chalky Studebaker,Boomer Bledsoe,Willie White.EnemiesRoger Klotz[Source]Constance "Connie" Bengeis a recurring character and one ofDoug Funnie's friends throughout the series. Connie is a sweet, naive, heavy-set girl in the Nickelodeon series. She seems to have a small crush on Doug. She once attempts to ask Doug out to a dance, but Doug invents a fake dying cousin in order to get out of it, so Patti can ask him. Eventually, he tells her the truth and she forgives him, telling him all of the times she lied for similar reasons. Roger and his gang enjoy tormenting Connie on occasion. She finally retaliated in the episode "Doug Throws a Party": after Roger yanked off the hat she was wearing and exposed her embarrassingly bad haircut, she punched Roger in the gut and yelled, "Why don't you grow up, you big baby?!", temporarily stunning everyone else in the room to silence until she exclaimed, "What are you all looking at?  Let's party!"

characters.name: Dale Valentine
characters.description: Dale ValentineCharacter informationBirthdayAugust 31, 1989 (age 32)GenderMaleInterestsPlayingRelativesSkeeter Valentine(Older Brother)Ruby Valentine(Mother)Joe Valentine(Father)[Source] Dale Valentineis the two-year-old brother ofSkeeter Valentine. He is voiced by Fred Newman. Dale Valentine is a well-mannered kid and gets along well with everybody, although he accidentally offendsDougwhen he calls him "Big Nose" in the episode, "Doug's Big Nose". Dale's biggest role in the entire series was in "Doug Loses Dale", where Doug babysits Dale and accidentally loses him right before Skeeter's parents come home. Even though he is still a toddler, Dale occasionally demonstrates signs of substantial intelligence, such as when he is the only person who recognizes Doug underneath theHamburger Boyoutfit in the episode "Doug is Hamburger Boy".

Douglas is a shy, insecure and somewhat clumsy 11 (12½ in the Disney version) year old boy with a wild imagination and a big heart. He’s in the 6th grade atBluffington School(7th grade atBeebe Bluff Middle Schoolin the Disney version). He owns a very expressive dog namedPorkchop.
